text.title: Agentic Code Review Pipeline
text.summary: Layered review pipeline: automated gate, summary agent, diff-reviewer agent, then human reviewer.

section.title: Pipeline Stages

rule.statement: Gate PRs with automated checks before human review: lint + format + typecheck + test + coverage + security scan. Human reviewers see only green PRs.
rule.rationale: Automated checks catch the mechanical issues (style, type errors, known vulnerabilities) that would distract a human reviewer. Keeping reviewers focused on logic and design yields higher quality feedback.

section.reference: GitHub Actions workflow: lint, test, coverage, PR size warning

list.item: Summary agent: posts 200-token PR summary (scope, files changed, risks, coverage gaps) on PR open.
Diff-reviewer agent: comments on hunks for missed nulls, hallucinated APIs, untested branches. Configured with project rubric. Never approves, only flags.
Human reviewer: focuses on architecture, API design, business logic — the concerns agents cannot judge.

rule.statement: Agent-reviewer must be configured with project conventions (`.agents/review-rubric.md`) to avoid false positives on project-specific patterns.
rule.rationale: Without a rubric, agent reviewers flag project-intentional patterns as bugs (e.g., "use type hints" on a project that deliberately avoids them), training engineers to ignore all agent feedback.

example.code: PR summary prompt: You are a senior reviewer. Read the PR diff and post: - Scope: 1 sentence. - Files changed: count + risky files (auth/, payments/, migrations/). - Tests: which behaviors covered? gaps? - Risks: list each non-obvious risk + line reference. - Out of scope: anything that should be a follow-up PR. Do NOT approve. Do NOT request changes unless unbuildable.

Diff review prompt: Review the diff against .agents/review-rubric.md. For each hunk output: - file:line - severity: blocker | major | minor | nit | praise - finding (1 sentence) - suggested patch (<=5 lines) Skip style issues (Prettier/ruff handles those). Stop after 30 findings; ask for follow-up if more.
